description: 50 curated test sentences for the LLM-as-rewriter vs AMR/UMR-LDA pilot study. Each sentence is annotated with applicable logical equivalence rules, anticipated LLM failure modes, and gold-standard rewrites where unambiguous. Sentences span 5 difficulty categories and 13 equivalence rules.

difficulty_categories.C1_basic_implication: Short, single if-then statements with binary predicates
difficulty_categories.C5_long_complex_prose: Multi-clause sentences from realistic prose, including known reversal-curse and modal-precision cases

sentences.text: If Alice studies hard, then she passes the exam.

gold_outputs.contraposition: If Alice does not pass the exam, then she does not study hard.
gold_outputs.implication: Alice does not study hard, or she passes the exam.

anticipated_llm_failures.contraposition: Replaces 'not pass' with 'fails' (lexical antonym substitution breaking exact polarity)

sentences.text: If the device is unplugged, the LED does not light up.

gold_outputs.contraposition: If the LED lights up, then the device is not unplugged.
gold_outputs.double_negation: If the device is not plugged in, the LED does not light up.

anticipated_llm_failures.contraposition: May produce 'If the LED lights up, the device is plugged in' — collapses double negation prematurely

sentences.text: If a triangle has three equal sides, then it has three equal angles.

gold_outputs.contraposition: If a triangle does not have three equal angles, then it does not have three equal sides.

anticipated_llm_failures.contraposition: May insert mathematical equivalence reasoning rather than perform syntactic contraposition

sentences.text: If the water reaches 100 degrees Celsius at sea level, then it boils.

gold_outputs.contraposition: If the water does not boil, then it does not reach 100 degrees Celsius at sea level.

anticipated_llm_failures.contraposition: Might add real-world qualifications ('assuming standard atmospheric pressure') that drift from the original logical form

sentences.text: If Mary owns a car, then she has a driver's license.

gold_outputs.contraposition: If Mary does not have a driver's license, then she does not own a car.
gold_outputs.implication: Mary does not own a car, or she has a driver's license.

sentences.text: If the building has an elevator, residents on upper floors are not required to take the stairs.

gold_outputs.contraposition: If residents on upper floors are required to take the stairs, then the building does not have an elevator.

anticipated_llm_failures.contraposition: Long predicate 'required to take the stairs' is prone to paraphrase that drops the 'required' modality

sentences.text: If a meeting is scheduled for Friday, then no team members are available for travel that day.

gold_outputs.contraposition: If some team members are available for travel on Friday, then no meeting is scheduled for Friday.

anticipated_llm_failures.contraposition: Negation of 'no team members are available' often collapsed incorrectly to 'all team members are available' instead of 'some team members are available'

sentences.text: If a patient takes the medication and follows the doctor's instructions, then they recover within two weeks.

gold_outputs.contraposition: If a patient does not recover within two weeks, then they did not take the medication or did not follow the doctor's instructions.

anticipated_llm_failures.contraposition: Often produces 'did not take the medication and did not follow instructions' — fails to apply De Morgan when negating the conjoined antecedent

sentences.text: The bald eagle is clever and the wolf is fierce.

gold_outputs.commutative: The wolf is fierce and the bald eagle is clever.

anticipated_llm_failures.commutative: LLM may apply unnecessary lexical paraphrase ('cunning' for 'clever') changing semantic content beyond the rule

sentences.text: Either the report is filed by Monday or the deadline extension is approved.

gold_outputs.commutative: Either the deadline extension is approved or the report is filed by Monday.
gold_outputs.implication: If the report is not filed by Monday, then the deadline extension is approved.

anticipated_llm_failures.implication: May treat 'either/or' as exclusive disjunction and add 'but not both' constraint absent in the original

sentences.text: The athlete trains every morning and competes on weekends.

gold_outputs.commutative: The athlete competes on weekends and trains every morning.

sentences.text: The contract is signed or the negotiation continues.

gold_outputs.commutative: The negotiation continues or the contract is signed.
gold_outputs.implication: If the contract is not signed, then the negotiation continues.

sentences.text: It is not the case that Alice is tall and Bob is short.

gold_outputs.de_morgan: Alice is not tall or Bob is not short.

anticipated_llm_failures.de_morgan: Often outputs 'Either Alice is not tall, or Bob is not short, but not both' — introduces XOR constraint absent in original; or outputs 'Alice is not tall and Bob is not short' — fails De Morgan entirely

sentences.text: Neither the manager nor the assistant attended the meeting.

gold_outputs.de_morgan: The manager did not attend the meeting and the assistant did not attend the meeting.

anticipated_llm_failures.de_morgan: May produce 'The manager and the assistant did not attend the meeting' — ambiguous between joint and individual readings

sentences.text: It is not true that the project is delayed or the budget is exceeded.

gold_outputs.de_morgan: The project is not delayed and the budget is not exceeded.

sentences.text: Either the patient has a fever and a cough, or the diagnosis is inconclusive.

gold_outputs.implication: If the patient does not have a fever and does not have a cough, or if either is absent, then the diagnosis is inconclusive. (Note: requires care with conjunction inside disjunction)

anticipated_llm_failures.implication: Most LLMs flatten 'fever and cough' to 'fever or cough' under negation, mangling the embedded conjunction scope

sentences.text: It is not the case that the suspect is not guilty.

anticipated_llm_failures.double_negation: Some LLMs preserve hedging: 'The suspect appears to be guilty' — drift from clean logical equivalence

sentences.text: It is false that Alice did not call Bob.
It is not impossible that the experiment will succeed.

gold_outputs.double_negation: It is possible that the experiment will succeed.
gold_outputs.modal_strength_inversion: The experiment is possible (UMR: modal-strength PrtAff vs FullNeg).

anticipated_llm_failures.double_negation: 'Not impossible' often rewritten as 'likely' — modal strength upgrade not warranted by pure double negation

sentences.text: If it is not the case that the alarm did not sound, then the security system did not malfunction.

gold_outputs.double_negation_then_contraposition: If the security system malfunctioned, then the alarm did not sound.

anticipated_llm_failures.any: Cascading negations confuse LLMs; common errors include dropping one of the negations or applying contraposition to the wrong clause

sentences.text: Every student loves some teacher.

gold_outputs.no_equivalent_rewrite: Surface-level reorderings risk changing scope. ∀x∃y vs ∃y∀x are not equivalent.

anticipated_llm_failures.scope_change: LLM often produces 'Some teacher is loved by every student' which strengthens to ∃y∀x — logically NOT equivalent
anticipated_llm_failures.commutative_misapplied: If asked to apply 'commutative law' on this, LLM may swap quantifiers

sentences.text: If every employee submits their timesheet, then payroll is processed.

gold_outputs.contraposition: If payroll is not processed, then some employee did not submit their timesheet.

anticipated_llm_failures.contraposition: Negation of universal often incorrectly given as 'no employees submitted' instead of 'some employee did not submit'

sentences.text: Some answer in the exam is incorrect.

gold_outputs.double_negation_check: Equivalent to: It is not the case that every answer in the exam is correct.

anticipated_llm_failures.quantifier_negation: May produce 'No answer is correct' which is much stronger than the original

sentences.text: All birds can fly or some birds can swim.

gold_outputs.commutative: Some birds can swim or all birds can fly.

anticipated_llm_failures.commutative: May 'correct' the factual content (penguins!) and rewrite content, not just reorder

sentences.text: Every cat is a mammal and every mammal is a vertebrate.

gold_outputs.commutative: Every mammal is a vertebrate and every cat is a mammal.

anticipated_llm_failures.transitivity: LLMs typically do this correctly when prompted, but in a multi-step proof setting often fail to identify when transitivity applies vs when an extra premise is needed

sentences.text: If a country produces more goods than it consumes, it exports the surplus to trading partners.

gold_outputs.contraposition: If a country does not export the surplus to trading partners, then it does not produce more goods than it consumes.

anticipated_llm_failures.contraposition: Comparative 'more than' is a frequent failure mode: negation 'does not produce more than' is often paraphrased as 'produces less than or equal to' — semantically close but lexically different

sentences.text: When the chairperson is unavailable, the vice chair conducts the meeting, and minutes are circulated within 24 hours.

gold_outputs.commutative: When the chairperson is unavailable, minutes are circulated within 24 hours, and the vice chair conducts the meeting.

anticipated_llm_failures.commutative: Temporal ordering of clauses is often interpreted as causal, leading to refusal or paraphrase that introduces 'after' or 'before'

sentences.text: If a software vulnerability is reported and the patch is delayed, then the system is at risk and the security team must be notified.

gold_outputs.contraposition: If the system is not at risk or the security team need not be notified, then either no software vulnerability is reported or the patch is not delayed.

anticipated_llm_failures.contraposition: Two conjunctions on both sides + modal verb 'must' make this extremely error-prone for LLMs; common failures: dropping De Morgan in one side, weakening 'must' to 'should'

sentences.text: Tom Cruise's mother is Mary Lee Pfeiffer.

gold_outputs.inverse_relation: Mary Lee Pfeiffer's son is Tom Cruise.

anticipated_llm_failures.reversal_curse_specific: GPT-4 can write the inverse correctly, but Berglund 2023 shows the model finetuned only on 'A is mother of B' still fails B→A queries — so even when the rewrite text is correct, embedding-level reversal fails. AMR/UMR rule provides explicit symbolic link.

sentences.text: Marie Curie discovered radium in 1898.

gold_outputs.inverse_relation: Radium was discovered by Marie Curie in 1898.
gold_outputs.tense_transformation: Marie Curie discovers radium in 1898. (historical present — UMR aspect distinction)

anticipated_llm_failures.passive_voice_change: Inverse relation here = passive voice; LLMs usually do this right but the systematic link between active and passive in semantic graphs is what we want, not pattern matching

sentences.text: The teacher praised the student.

gold_outputs.inverse_relation: The student was praised by the teacher.

sentences.text: Alice is married to Bob.

gold_outputs.symmetric: Bob is married to Alice. (predicate is symmetric)

anticipated_llm_failures.asymmetric_misapplied: Distinguishing 'is married to' (symmetric) from 'is the parent of' (asymmetric) requires predicate-typing knowledge that LLMs sometimes confuse

sentences.text: Carlos is taller than Diana.

gold_outputs.asymmetric_negation: Diana is not taller than Carlos.
gold_outputs.inverse_lex: Diana is shorter than Carlos. (lexical inverse, semantically equivalent if total ordering)

anticipated_llm_failures.inverse_relation_misapplied: LLM may produce 'Diana is taller than Carlos' if asked for an 'inverse' without understanding asymmetry

sentences.text: Beijing is the capital of China.

gold_outputs.asymmetric_note: 'capital of' is asymmetric — China is not the capital of Beijing

anticipated_llm_failures.reversal_curse_specific: Even though LLMs write this correctly, performance on 'What city is the capital of China?' vs 'Of which country is Beijing the capital?' diverges in low-resource fine-tuning

sentences.text: The dog barks.

gold_outputs.predicate_implication: The dog makes a sound. (barks ⊨ makes_sound via WordNet hypernym)

anticipated_llm_failures.predicate_implication: LLM usually does this correctly but inconsistent across predicates; AMR has principled WordNet linkage

sentences.text: The robin is a bird.

gold_outputs.predicate_implication: The robin is an animal. (bird ⊨ animal)

sentences.text: Alice ran a marathon yesterday.

gold_outputs.tense_only_naive: Alice runs a marathon yesterday. (ungrammatical — naive tense swap fails)
gold_outputs.tense_aspect_proper_UMR: Alice completed a marathon yesterday. (UMR aspect: performance, telic; tense: past)

anticipated_llm_failures.tense_transformation: LLM rewrites with shifting aspect (e.g., 'runs' → progressive 'is running') without principled control over UMR's aspect category

sentences.text: Alice has been studying for the bar exam.

gold_outputs.aspect_activity: Alice is engaged in studying for the bar exam. (UMR aspect: activity, atelic ongoing)

anticipated_llm_failures.aspect_drift: LLM may paraphrase to 'Alice has studied' (perfect) or 'Alice studies' (habitual), shifting UMR aspect category from activity to performance/habitual

sentences.text: Trains depart from this station every hour on weekdays.

gold_outputs.aspect_habitual: Hourly train departures from this station are a weekday norm. (UMR aspect: habitual)

anticipated_llm_failures.aspect_drift: LLM may switch to single-event reading ('A train departs every hour'), losing the habitual quantification

sentences.text: Alice must finish her homework before dinner.

gold_outputs.modal_inversion: It is not the case that Alice may skip her homework before dinner. (UMR modal-strength: FullAff → not FullNeg)

anticipated_llm_failures.modal_weakening: Common output: 'Alice should finish her homework before dinner' — this weakens FullAff to PrtAff, NOT logically equivalent

sentences.text: Visitors may use the WiFi after registering at the front desk.

gold_outputs.modal_inversion: It is permitted, but not required, for visitors to use the WiFi after registering. (UMR modal-strength: PrtAff)

anticipated_llm_failures.modal_drift: LLM may rewrite as 'Visitors can use WiFi after registering' (modal-strength shift may → can, often FullAff in LLM interpretation)

sentences.text: The conference cannot be held without proper accreditation.

gold_outputs.modal_inversion_double_neg: The conference requires proper accreditation. (cannot...without = FullNeg of FullNeg = FullAff requirement)

anticipated_llm_failures.modal_weakening: Common output: 'The conference should have proper accreditation' — should is PrtAff, weaker than FullAff requirement

sentences.text: After the manager approved the budget, the team began the project.

gold_outputs.temporal_explicit: Event A: manager approved budget. Event B: team began project. Relation: A :before B

anticipated_llm_failures.temporal_ambiguity: When chained with another temporal statement, LLM often fails to compose: A before B + B before C ⇒ A before C is not reliably done in multi-step prompts

sentences.text: The flight took off after the weather cleared, which happened after the storm passed.

gold_outputs.temporal_chain: storm passed :before weather cleared :before flight took off (transitivity: storm passed :before flight took off)

anticipated_llm_failures.temporal_resolution: LLM may misorder events when relative clause introduces non-default order; explicit UMR :before annotation is unambiguous

sentences.text: If every passenger boards by 8 AM and the crew is ready, then the flight departs at 8:15 AM.

gold_outputs.contraposition_with_de_morgan: If the flight does not depart at 8:15 AM, then either some passenger has not boarded by 8 AM, or the crew is not ready.

anticipated_llm_failures.combined_failure: Combines quantifier negation, De Morgan, contraposition, and temporal anchor — failure rate cascades multiplicatively

sentences.text: The CEO announced the merger, and the stock price rose by 12 percent.

gold_outputs.commutative_naive: The stock price rose by 12 percent and the CEO announced the merger. (warning: surface-commutative but implies different causal/temporal reading)
gold_outputs.umr_preserved: Commutativity holds only at logical level; UMR doc-level temporal :before is preserved separately, so the rule outputs both reorderings as logically equivalent while marking temporal order independently

anticipated_llm_failures.causal_pragmatics: LLM refuses to commute when sensing implicit causality, or rewrites with 'after' — both deviate from logical commutativity

sentences.text: Marie Curie was awarded the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1903, and she was awarded the Nobel Prize in Chemistry in 1911.

gold_outputs.inverse_per_conjunct: The Nobel Prize in Physics was awarded to Marie Curie in 1903, and the Nobel Prize in Chemistry was awarded to her in 1911.
gold_outputs.commutative_with_temporal_preserved: Marie Curie was awarded the Nobel Prize in Chemistry in 1911, and she was awarded the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1903. (logical commutativity OK; temporal order changed)

anticipated_llm_failures.reversal_curse_compound: Multiple inverse-relation transforms in sequence; reversal-curse research suggests downstream retrieval performance still degrades for B→A even when text is correct

sentences.text: It is mandatory that the report not be released until the audit is complete.

gold_outputs.modal_temporal: The report must wait until the audit completes before release. UMR: modal-strength=FullAff on prohibition; temporal :after(audit_complete, report_release)

anticipated_llm_failures.modal_temporal_mixing: LLM commonly conflates 'mandatory not' with 'not mandatory', and temporal 'until' anchor is rewritten as 'when' — losing strict ordering

sentences.text: If a research finding is significant, it gets published in a top journal, and its citation count grows over time.

gold_outputs.contraposition_partial: If a research finding does not get published in a top journal or its citation count does not grow over time, then the finding is not significant.

anticipated_llm_failures.consequent_decomposition: When the consequent itself is a conjunction with temporal progression, LLMs tend to negate only the first conjunct, losing the temporal claim

sentences.text: It is not the case that, if Alice is not invited, then Bob will not attend the party.

gold_outputs.implication_then_de_morgan: Alice is not invited, and Bob attends the party.

anticipated_llm_failures.negated_implication: Negation of an implication is a famous logic confusion; LLMs often produce 'Alice is not invited and Bob does not attend' (preserving outer negation incorrectly) or 'If Alice is invited, Bob attends' (collapsing to wrong direction)
